testcase.name: createAnnouncement > returns 401 when req.user is missing
createAnnouncement > returns 400 when title is missing
createAnnouncement > returns 400 when announcement message is missing
createAnnouncement > returns 400 when recipients list is empty
createAnnouncement > returns 201 and commits on a successful announcement
createAnnouncement > returns 500 and calls ROLLBACK when INSERT returns no id
createAnnouncement > calls ROLLBACK and rethrows when a DB error occurs mid-transaction
createAnnouncement > inserts a brochure file row when a brochure is uploaded
createAnnouncement > de-duplicates recipient ids before inserting
listMyAnnouncements > returns 401 when req.user is missing
listMyAnnouncements > returns the announcements list for the authenticated user
listMyAnnouncements > uses default limit of 50 when no limit param is supplied
listMyAnnouncements > clamps limit to a maximum of 200 (bug fix)
listMyAnnouncements > enforces a minimum limit of 1
listMyAnnouncements > honours a valid limit within the allowed range
listMyAnnouncements > returns 500 on DB error
verifyOTP > returns 400 when email is missing
verifyOTP > returns 400 when otp is missing
verifyOTP > returns 401 when no OTP record exists for the email
verifyOTP > returns 429 when the OTP has exceeded 5 attempts
verifyOTP > returns 401 when the OTP is expired
verifyOTP > returns 401 and increments attempts when OTP code is wrong
verifyOTP > returns 500 when user row is missing after successful OTP verification (bug fix)
verifyOTP > returns 200 with a JWT and session token on successful verification
loginVerifyOTP > returns 400 when email is missing
loginVerifyOTP > returns 400 when otp is missing
loginVerifyOTP > returns 401 when no OTP record exists
loginVerifyOTP > returns 429 when too many failed OTP attempts
loginVerifyOTP > returns 401 when OTP is expired
loginVerifyOTP > returns 401 for a wrong OTP code
loginVerifyOTP > returns 500 when user row is missing after OTP deletion (bug fix)
loginVerifyOTP > returns 200 with JWT on successful login OTP verification
listHackathons — limit / offset clamping > defaults limit to 20 when no limit param is provided
listHackathons — limit / offset clamping > defaults offset to 0 when no offset param is provided
listHackathons — limit / offset clamping > uses a valid limit within 1–200 as-is
listHackathons — limit / offset clamping > clamps limit to 200 when a value above 200 is supplied (bug fix)
listHackathons — limit / offset clamping > falls back to default limit of 20 when limit=0 is supplied (0 is falsy)
listHackathons — limit / offset clamping > enforces a minimum limit of 1 for truthy negative values (e.g. limit=-5)
listHackathons — limit / offset clamping > clamps negative offset to 0 (bug fix)
listHackathons — limit / offset clamping > uses a positive offset as-is
listHackathons — limit / offset clamping > falls back to limit=20 when limit is non-numeric
listHackathons — response shape > returns an object with a hackathons array
listHackathons — response shape > returns 500 on a DB error
requireAuth > no auth headers > returns 401 'No token' when both Authorization and x-session-token are absent
requireAuth > session-token path > returns 401 'Invalid session' when verifySession returns null
requireAuth > session-token path > returns 401 'User not found' when session is valid but user not in DB
testcase.system-out: {"@timestamp":"2026-04-19T03:10:44.558Z","db.statement":"SELECT id, role FROM users WHERE id = $1","ecs.version":"8.10.0","log.level":"debug","message":"db.query.start","service.environment":"test","service.name":"drms-backend","service.version":"1.0.0"} {"@timestamp":"2026-04-19T03:10:44.561Z","db.statement":"SELECT id, role FROM users WHERE id = $1","ecs.version":"8.10.0","event.duration_ms":4.23,"log.level":"info","message":"db.query.complete","service.environment":"test","service.name":"drms-backend","service.version":"1.0.0"}

testcase.name: requireAuth > session-token path > sets req.user, calls next() and extends session on valid session token
testcase.system-out: {"@timestamp":"2026-04-19T03:10:44.562Z","db.statement":"SELECT id, role FROM users WHERE id = $1","ecs.version":"8.10.0","log.level":"debug","message":"db.query.start","service.environment":"test","service.name":"drms-backend","service.version":"1.0.0"} {"@timestamp":"2026-04-19T03:10:44.562Z","db.statement":"SELECT id, role FROM users WHERE id = $1","ecs.version":"8.10.0","event.duration_ms":0.21,"log.level":"info","message":"db.query.complete","service.environment":"test","service.name":"drms-backend","service.version":"1.0.0"}

testcase.name: requireAuth > session-token path > returns 401 when session verification throws
requireAuth > Bearer token path > returns 401 'Invalid token format' when Authorization header has no token after 'Bearer '
requireAuth > Bearer token path > calls next() and sets req.user for a valid Bearer JWT without sid
requireAuth > Bearer token path > returns 401 'Token expired' for an expired JWT
requireAuth > Bearer token path > returns 401 'Invalid token' for a malformed JWT
requireAuth > Bearer token path > returns 401 'Session revoked' when JWT has sid but session is no longer active
requireAuth > Bearer token path > calls next() when JWT has sid and session is still active
optionalAuth > calls next() without setting req.user when no auth headers are present
optionalAuth > sets req.user and calls next() for a valid session token
testcase.system-out: {"@timestamp":"2026-04-19T03:10:44.569Z","db.statement":"SELECT id, role FROM users WHERE id = $1","ecs.version":"8.10.0","log.level":"debug","message":"db.query.start","service.environment":"test","service.name":"drms-backend","service.version":"1.0.0"} {"@timestamp":"2026-04-19T03:10:44.569Z","db.statement":"SELECT id, role FROM users WHERE id = $1","ecs.version":"8.10.0","event.duration_ms":0.35,"log.level":"info","message":"db.query.complete","service.environment":"test","service.name":"drms-backend","service.version":"1.0.0"}

testcase.name: optionalAuth > sets req.user and calls next() for a valid Bearer JWT
optionalAuth > returns 401 'Token expired' for expired JWT in optionalAuth
request log — url.path > logs the full originalUrl path, not the router-scoped req.path
request log — url.path > strips query string from url.path
response log — url.path > logs the full originalUrl path on finish, not the router-scoped req.path
response log — url.path > logs the full path for nested routes like /api/announcements/mine
response log — url.path > request and response logs record the same url.path
abort log — url.path > logs the full originalUrl path when the client disconnects before a response
correlation ID > reuses a valid client-supplied X-Correlation-ID
correlation ID > generates a new UUID when the supplied correlation ID is invalid
response log level > uses '200' level for HTTP NaN
response log level > uses '302' level for HTTP NaN
response log level > uses '400' level for HTTP NaN
response log level > uses '404' level for HTTP NaN
response log level > uses '500' level for HTTP NaN
response log level > uses '503' level for HTTP NaN
requireRole middleware > calls next() when the user has an allowed role
requireRole middleware > returns 403 when the user's role is not in the allowed list
requireRole middleware > returns 401 when req.user is undefined
requireRole middleware > allows any role listed in the allowed array
requireRole middleware > rejects a role not in a multi-role allowed list
requireRole middleware > rejects when allowedRoles is empty
validate middleware > calls next() and replaces req.body with sanitized value on valid input
validate middleware > returns 400 with errors array on invalid input
validate middleware > collects all validation errors (abortEarly: false)
validate middleware > strips unknown fields from req.body (stripUnknown: true)
validate middleware > coerces string numbers to numbers (convert: true)
validate middleware > handles undefined req.body gracefully (defaults to {})
validate middleware > error details include field path and message
ReviewError > is an instance of Error
ReviewError > stores the HTTP status code
ReviewError > stores the message
reviewProject > returns success message on approve
reviewProject > returns success message on reject
reviewProject > passes 'approved' status to DB on approve action
reviewProject > passes 'rejected' status to DB on reject action
reviewProject > sends an email notification to the project creator on approve
reviewProject > sends an email notification on reject
reviewProject > throws ReviewError(403) when staff is not an activity coordinator
reviewProject > throws ReviewError(404) when project is not found
reviewProject > skips email when creator has no email row in the DB
reviewProject > skips email when created_by is null/undefined
reviewProject > does not throw when email sending fails (logs error instead)
reviewAchievement > returns success message on approve
reviewAchievement > returns success message on reject
reviewAchievement > passes 'approved' status and verified=true to DB on approve
reviewAchievement > passes 'rejected' status and verified=false to DB on reject
reviewAchievement > sends email to achievement owner on approve
reviewAchievement > throws ReviewError(403) when staff is not an activity coordinator
reviewAchievement > throws ReviewError(404) when achievement is not found
reviewAchievement > does not throw when email sending fails (logs error instead)
reviewAchievement > uses null for comment when none is provided
otpGenerator > generateOTP > returns a string
otpGenerator > generateOTP > returns exactly 6 characters
otpGenerator > generateOTP > contains only digit characters
otpGenerator > generateOTP > is in the range 100000–999999
otpGenerator > generateOTP > produces different values on repeated calls (probabilistic)
otpGenerator > getExpiryDate > returns a Date object
otpGenerator > getExpiryDate > defaults to 5 minutes from now
otpGenerator > getExpiryDate > accepts a custom minutes value
otpGenerator > getExpiryDate > returns a date in the future
QueryBuilder > build() with only a base returns just the base SQL
QueryBuilder > addWhere adds a WHERE clause for a single condition
QueryBuilder > multiple addWhere calls are joined with AND
QueryBuilder > addJoin inserts a JOIN clause before the WHERE block
QueryBuilder > addParam returns sequential placeholders $1, $2, ...
QueryBuilder > addParam populates the values array in order
QueryBuilder > build(suffix) appends suffix after WHERE
QueryBuilder > build(suffix) without WHERE still appends suffix
QueryBuilder > addJoin and addWhere return this for fluent chaining
QueryBuilder > does not emit WHERE keyword when no conditions added
QueryBuilder > param placeholders in JOINs and WHEREs reference correct values
sessionUtils — pure functions > generateSessionToken > returns a 64-character hexadecimal string
sessionUtils — pure functions > generateSessionToken > returns unique values across consecutive calls
sessionUtils — pure functions > getSessionExpiryDate > returns a Date instance
sessionUtils — pure functions > getSessionExpiryDate > returns a date approximately 90 days in the future
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > createSession > calls pool.query and returns the created session row
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > createSession > stores null for deviceInfo when not provided
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > createSession > serialises deviceInfo as JSON when provided
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > createSession > propagates DB errors
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > verifySession > returns the session row when found
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > verifySession > returns null when no matching session exists
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > verifySession > propagates DB errors
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > extendSession > returns the updated session when token is valid
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > extendSession > returns null when token is not found or inactive
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > getUserActiveSessions > returns array of active sessions for a user
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > getUserActiveSessions > returns empty array when user has no active sessions
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > hasValidSession > returns true when a valid session exists
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > hasValidSession > returns false when no valid session exists
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > invalidateSession > returns true when a session was successfully invalidated
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > invalidateSession > returns false when token was not found
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > invalidateAllUserSessions > returns the number of sessions invalidated
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > invalidateAllUserSessions > returns 0 when user had no sessions
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > cleanupExpiredSessions > returns the count of deleted sessions
sessionUtils — DB functions (pool mocked) > cleanupExpiredSessions > issues a DELETE query
tokenUtils > signToken > produces a non-empty JWT string
tokenUtils > signToken > encodes the payload in the token
tokenUtils > signToken > respects a custom expiresIn value
tokenUtils > signToken > defaults expiry to 1 hour
tokenUtils > verifyToken > returns the decoded payload for a valid token
tokenUtils > verifyToken > throws TokenExpiredError for an expired token
tokenUtils > verifyToken > throws JsonWebTokenError for a tampered token
tokenUtils > verifyToken > throws for a completely invalid string
